Scientists Around the World Call to Protect Research on One of Humanity's Greatest Short-term Threats: Disinformation - Tech Policy Press
snippet: Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino is listed among scientists around the world calling to protect research on disinformation.
description: This Tech Policy Press article lists Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino as one of the scientists calling to protect research on disinformation. The article focuses on the importance of disinformation research, which aligns with his research interests.

title: NEWS - Urbino - Mapping Italian News Research Program - Uniurb
snippet: Mentions Prof. Fabio Giglietto as Internet Studies professor and chair of vera.ai WP4 and contains contact information. Also mentions a white paper co-authored by Fabio Giglietto and Massimo Terenzi from the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, which explores how the Digital Services Act (DSA) is transforming data access for platform research.
description: The Uniurb news page mentions Prof. Fabio Giglietto as an Internet Studies professor and chair of vera.ai WP4, also highlighting his co-authored white paper on the DSA and data access for platform research, which strongly aligns with his expertise.

title: Meta shuts down CrowdTangle tool used by some groups to track online misinformation
snippet: Dr. Fabio Giglietto, an associate professor of internet studies at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo in Italy, said that he anticipated many researchers would voice their disappointment over the end of CrowdTangle. He also cited difficulties with running queries on the replacement tool, Meta Content Library.
description: This article discusses Meta's decision to shut down CrowdTangle, a tool used for tracking online misinformation, and quotes Fabio Giglietto, an associate professor of internet studies, regarding the impact on researchers.

title: Meta shuts down CrowdTangle tool used by some groups to track online misinformation - The Straits Times
description: News article from The Straits Times

title: Meta Is Getting Rid of CrowdTangle — and Its Replacement Isn't As Transparent or Accessible - Proof News
snippet: Fabio Giglietto, an associate professor of internet studies at the University of Urbino, is quoted regarding the limitations of Meta's MCL as a replacement for CrowdTangle. He mentions restrictions on data exporting and difficulties in performing tasks he could previously do with CrowdTangle. He also mentioned the @FacebooksTop10 bot as an example of something that would no longer be possible with the MCL.
description: The article discusses Meta's decision to replace CrowdTangle with MCL and quotes Fabio Giglietto, an associate professor of internet studies at the University of Urbino, regarding the limitations of the new tool. Giglietto's quote focuses on the restrictions and difficulties compared to CrowdTangle, particularly regarding data exporting and the ability to perform certain analyses.
